name: debug-and-fix
description: Drop your repo, get working code back. Scans the codebase, runs tests, parses errors, finds root cause, generates fixes, verifies with tests, reports before/after. Pure flow pipeline — no runtime dependencies.

config.instruction: Scan the project: detect language, framework, test runner, project structure, entry points, and dependencies. Output a structured codebase map.
Run the test suite to capture baseline state. Record total tests, passing, failing, error messages with stack traces. This is the BEFORE snapshot.
Analyze the failing tests. Identify root cause: which file, which function, what went wrong, and why. Produce fix hints.
Generate a minimal fix based on the root cause analysis. Apply it. Keep the change as small as possible — fewer lines modified is better.
Run the test suite again after the fix. Record results. Compare with the BEFORE snapshot. If tests still fail, flag which ones and why.
Generate a before/after report: tests fixed, regressions, diff summary, confidence score. Format as a verification checklist with pass/fail for each item.
